title: Q1 2026 - Brand New Apps!

posts.author: bridieca (Bridie Castiel)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is your spot to stay up to date with all of the latest amazing apps launched in the Apps Marketplace this quarter!

Apps Marketplace partners - please only share newly launched apps, or new features added to existing apps from Jan - March.

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone!

Mirror Item Multiple Boards has had a big glow-up over the past month, and it’s now even easier to work with the same item across multiple boards — including updates and subitems, fully in sync.

If you’re new to the app: it lets you mirror one item to multiple boards and keep everything aligned, so teams don’t have to jump between boards or duplicate work.

151920×960 253 KB

What’s new (Jan–March):

  • Visual emoji indicators to instantly see which items are mirrored

  • One-way syncing for tighter control over how data flows

  • New automation templates, including:
    When a person is assigned → mirror the item to a target board & group

  • Update source tagging – every synced update clearly shows which board it came from

These updates make cross-board collaboration clearer, more controlled, and way easier to manage at scale.

Happy to answer any questions or hear feedback from anyone using it!

Feel free to reach out at support@fortimus.co.

posts.author: GanttGo (Hidai Bar-Mor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone!

Just launched GanttGo in the Marketplace. It’s a Gantt chart board view focused on one thing:
exporting project timelines you can actually share with clients.

What it does:

Multi-page PDF/Excel/PowerPoint export with dependency arrows preserved

Drag-to-create dependencies visually

Drag task bars to edit dates, synced back to Monday in real-time

Critical path highlighting

Holiday presets (US, UK, EU, German, French) + custom dates

Subitem support with proper indentation

Dark mode

Free tier: 50 items. Pro: 1000 items, custom logos, scheduled exports.

Would love feedback from anyone who tries it!

Search “GanttGo” in the Marketplace

Hidai, GanttGo Developer

posts.author: dvdsmpsn (David @ David Simpson Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Screenshot 2026-02-02 at 22.27.56998×172 13 KB

This last month has been very busy for our Microsoft 365 SharePoint & Outlook integration for monday.com.

So, what’s new this month?

tl;dr: Absolutely loads! Read on…

Outlook Calendar workflows

  • Create event in personal calendar
  • Create event in group calendar
  • Delete calendar events for item

SharePoint workflows

  • Create SharePoint site from item in monday.com
    • This complements the existing SharePoint file management workflows.

Import from Excel

  • Improved date/time support, based on the automation owner’s own specified timezone

Support for Sidekick AI

  • Extract data from Excel spreadsheets
    • This skill allows you to read from an Excel spreadsheet, extract only the precise data you need, and then (for example) add or update items within monday.com
  • Extract data from Word documents
    • This skill extracts data from Word documents stored in SharePoint and populates column values from the data found.
  • Extract data from Outlook email conversations
    • This skill allows you to import email conversations from Outlook and add them as item updates.
    • This complements the existing workflow to import Outlook emails into monday CRM activities

Improved onboarding

  • We’ve also massively improved our onboarding experience, so it’s never been easier to make monday.com work with Microsoft 365.

App listed on the Microsoft Marketplace

  • Microsoft approved our app listing here:
    Microsoft Marketplace | cloud solutions, AI apps, and agents

As you can see from all the new features added in just one month, we are very actively expanding the features and capabilities of this already very powerful app.

If you use both monday.com and Microsoft 365, we really think this is one app to rule them all.

View the docs or book a demo today

posts.author: hackercules (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone!

I’m happy to present Split to groups application which helps moving items to the multiple groups at once using item column value as target group name.

It could help after import which currently creates items only in the single new group or for the board reorganization: you can calculate new Groups in Formula column and app will move items where they should be.

posts.author: CarbonApps (CarbonApps)
posts.cookedHtml:
CarbonApps Communit Post1816×953 185 KB

Hi monday community!

We’re excited to introduce Automation Scheduler, a new app built by CarbonApps that adds something monday doesn’t currently support natively:

Scheduling Bulk Filtered Automations.

Most monday automations are reactive. They run when something changes on an item. That works well, but it also means boards slowly drift over time. Overdue items pile up, timers keep running, and stale statuses slip through unless someone manually cleans things up.

Automation Scheduler takes a different approach.

Instead of waiting for triggers, Automation Scheduler:

  • Runs on a schedule you define
  • Scans your entire board
  • Evaluates conditions across all items
  • Takes action in bulk on every matched item

This makes it possible to do things like:

  • Daily overdue task handling
  • Automatically stopping running timers
  • Weekly board cleanup and archiving
  • Flagging items that haven’t been updated in days
  • Scheduled follow-ups on deals or client work
  • + hundreds of other use cases

All without touching a single status, using convoluted workarounds, or relying on an item-level trigger.

If you’re responsible for board health, workflow governance, or operational consistency on monday, this fills a big gap between reactive automations and true system-wide control.

Schedule a demo with our team, and we’ll show you how it works!

The app is currently free to use while in our early release phase, so we encourage you to use it as much as possible and share your feedback!

Happy to answer questions or hear how you’d use scheduled automation in your workflow.

posts.author: andieatclarityco (Andie Bryan)
posts.cookedHtml: This is awesome! I’ll be sharing with my team this week to see how they would use this new app with our clients

posts.author: PermitNerd (Percy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey monday community

We just launched PermitNerd in the Marketplace.

It’s built specifically for construction and development teams who use monday.com but still track building permits outside their workflow.

Most teams manage permits through city portals, spreadsheets, and email threads — which makes it hard to keep permit status visible once a job is underway.

PermitNerd lets you:

Search real building permit data across supported cities

Pin permit records directly to your monday boards

Keep permit status visible alongside your project tasks

Reduce reliance on external portals during active construction

The goal is simple:

Bring permit visibility into monday instead of reacting when something stalls.

We’re early and actively improving based on feedback. If you manage projects where permits impact timelines, we’d love your thoughts.

You can find it by searching “PermitNerd” in the monday Marketplace.

Happy to answer any questions or hear how you’re currently tracking permits inside monday.

11920×960 225 KB

posts.author: anowacka (Anna from SolDevelo)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey guys, we just added a Timeline column support to our Ganttly - Hourly Gantt & Export. It means, you can generate your Gantt based on the timeline column and export it to CSV, PNG or high resolution SVG.

posts.author: i-jaglan (Sagar Jaglan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey monday community

We recently added Rollup Sync to SubItems Pro and it’s been a game-changer for teams managing subitems.

If you’ve ever wished subitem data would automatically roll up to the parent item, that’s exactly what this does.

SubItems Pro helps you:

Bulk create subitems across multiple items at once (up to 50 per item)

Inherit column values from parent items automatically (18 column types supported)

Clone subitems from one item to many — with or without column values

Bulk edit subitem columns across all selected items in one go

Rollup Sync — auto-sync subitem values to parent items via webhooks (SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX, COUNT, EARLIEST,
LATEST, SPAN, and more)

The Rollup feature supports Numbers, Status, Date, and Timeline columns — so you can roll up totals, find
earliest/latest dates, or span timelines automatically. Set it up once, and it runs in the background.

Whether you’re a PM managing tasks, a construction team tracking deliverables, or an agency juggling client projects if you work with subitems, this saves hours of manual updates.

Search “SubItems Pro” in the monday Marketplace to try it out.

Happy to answer any questions!

posts.author: DocCreate (FlowTech Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
DocCreate Update — E-Signatures, Cloud Drive Sync & Bulk Downloads

Hey monday Community

We’re excited to share some important new upgrades to DocCreate in 2026 that make document workflows faster and more connected to your existing file systems.

What’s New

E-Signature Support
You can now generate documents and send them for signature as part of your monday.com workflow, helping teams move faster on proposals, agreements, approvals, and operational documents.

Cloud Drive Sync
Automatically sync and store generated files in your preferred cloud storage:

  • Google Drive

  • OneDrive

  • SharePoint

This allows teams to maintain structured document storage aligned with their existing company file architecture.

Bulk File Downloads
Quickly download multiple generated documents at once — ideal for reporting, audits, project handovers, inspections, and large-scale operational workflows.

These upgrades are designed to reduce manual steps and make document automation more scalable inside monday.com.

We’d love your feedback from the community — feel free to share how you are using DocCreate in your workflows.

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community,

We researched user experience with the Gantt chart and noticed a repeated pattern:

Export Gantt → attach PDF → send email → wait for feedback → update timeline → export again → resend.

This workflow is familiar — almost a habit. But it isn’t very effective. That exploration led us to build Interactive Gantt Timeline — a way to keep the schedule live while maintaining full control over changes.

13231920×960 243 KB

What changes in practice

  • Comment and discuss directly on the Gantt
  • Let clients suggest timeline changes visually (no monday.com account required)
  • Test how changes impact the timeline before approving
  • Approve or reject changes and sync them back to monday automatically

If this sounds painfully familiar, you can install the app and test a new collaboration experience.

P.S. Built by Stiltsoft — a team developing and supporting business apps for nearly 15 years.
